prompt: You recently moved into a new apartment. Write a letter to your friend. In your letter: explain where you moved, describe one problem with the apartment, and invite your friend to visit.
sampleText:
Dear Tom,

I tell you I move new apartment last week. It is in north part of town, near big road and many small shop. The building is old but the rent not very high, so I take it because my work place is not far now. I can go by bus and it need only twenty minute.

But I have one big problem in apartment. The window in bedroom is broken and cold air come inside every night. I put paper and tape on it but still cold. Also when rain come, little water come on floor. I told landlord but he say later, later, not fix now. It make me angry and I sleep not very good.

You can visit me next Sunday if you have time. I want show you the place and maybe we cook dinner. Please call me before you come because I need clean room first.

Your friend,
Lin

prompt: You lost your student card. Write a letter to the college office. In your letter: explain what happened, say why you need the card, and ask what you should do.
sampleText:
Dear college office,

I am student in English class and I lost my card yesterday. I think it happen when I go to library after lunch. I take out my notebook and maybe student card fall on table or on floor. After that I go to bus stop and supermarket, but I not see card anymore. I try to find in my bag many time.

This card is very important for me because I use it to go inside college building and use library computer. Teacher also ask card when we take exam, so I worry very much. Next week I have test and maybe without card I cannot go into room. I also need borrow book for homework.

Please tell me what I must do. Can I get new card this week? I can bring photo and passport if you need. I can pay money also. Please answer me soon because I am very worried.

Thank you,
Mei Chen

prompt: Some people think children should start learning a foreign language at primary school. Do you agree or disagree?
sampleText:
Many people say children need learn foreign language in primary school. I agree because language is important now. If children learn English or other language, they can talk with people from other country. They can also get better job when they grow up. In my country many company want people speak English, so children learn early is good.

Children also remember word easy. When they are small, they are not afraid to speak. They can sing song and play game in foreign language. This is interesting for them. If they start in high school, maybe they are shy and they think language is difficult. So primary school can be better time.

But there are some problem. Children already have many lesson, such as math and science. If school put more language, children may feel tired. Some school also not have good teacher. If teacher cannot speak good language, children learn wrong pronunciation. Parents also maybe need pay more money for book and class.

I think foreign language should be taught in primary school, but not too much. Maybe two or three short lessons every week is enough. Teacher should use games, pictures and simple speaking, not hard exam. In this way children can enjoy language and not feel too much pressure.

In conclusion, I agree with learning language early because it is useful, but school must make it simple. Children should learn slowly and happily, not with too much homework. For example, my cousin learned English when he was young. Now he can read some story and speak with tourists. It show early learning can help, even if language is not perfect.

prompt: Some people say public transport should be free for everyone. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
sampleText:
Some people think bus and train should be free for all people. This idea has good side. Poor people can go to work, school and hospital without worry about money. In many city transport is expensive, so if it is free, life become easy. Also more people may use bus and train, so road have less car. This can make air better and traffic not very bad.

But other people think public transport cannot be free. Government need money to pay driver, petrol, train, station and repair. If nobody pay ticket, government must use tax. Maybe tax become high and people not happy. Free transport also may be very crowded. If too many people use it, bus is full and dirty. Some people may use it for no reason and make problem.

In my opinion, public transport should not be free for everyone. It is better to make it cheap. Student, old people and poor people can have free ticket or discount. Working people can pay small money because they use transport every day. Tourist can pay normal price. This is fairer.

Also government should improve service. If bus is late or dirty, people still use car even if bus is free. Good transport need clean buses, safe stations and enough time table. So money is still important.

To conclude, free public transport sounds good, but it can cost too much. I think cheap transport with help for poor people is better than free for everybody. In my city, many people use bus only when ticket is cheap. If price is too high, they choose motorbike or car. So lower price can still help many normal workers.

prompt: You bought a phone online and it does not work properly. Write a letter to the company. In your letter: describe the problem, explain how you feel, and say what you want the company to do.
sampleText:
Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing about a mobile phone I bought from your online shop two weeks ago. The order number is 45891. When the phone arrived, it looked new, but after I used it for one day, I found several problems.

First, the screen sometimes becomes black for two or three minutes. I cannot answer calls when this happen. Second, the battery is very weak. I charged the phone all night, but in the afternoon it had no power. I also tried another charger, but it was same problem. I think this phone is not good quality.

I feel very disappointed because I paid a lot of money. I need this phone for work and for contacting my family. Now I must use my old phone again, and this causes many trouble.

I want your company to send me a new phone or return my money. Please tell me how I can send this broken phone back to you. I hope you can answer soon.

Yours faithfully,
Chen Wei

prompt: You are going to miss an appointment with your dentist. Write a letter to the dentist. In your letter: apologise, explain why you cannot come, and ask for another appointment.
sampleText:
Dear Dr Smith,

I am very sorry but I cannot come to my dental appointment on Monday morning at ten o'clock. I know I should tell you earlier, but I just found out today that I must go to another city for my company meeting. My manager asked me to travel there on Sunday night, so I will not be in town on Monday.

I feel bad about this because I know your clinic is busy and you prepared time for me. I also still have some tooth pain, so I really need to see you. But this work meeting is important and I cannot refuse my manager.

Could you please give me another appointment next week? I am free on Wednesday afternoon after two o'clock, or on Friday morning before eleven. If these times are not possible, please tell me another time and I will try to change my work.

Thank you for your understanding. I am sorry again for the problem.

Yours sincerely,
David Wong

prompt: Some people believe that young people should spend more time helping in their local community. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
sampleText:
Many people think young people should help more in their local community. I agree with this idea because it is good for society and also good for young people themselves. Today many young people spend a lot of time on phone or computer. If they help in community, they can learn real life and meet different people.

There are many things young people can do. For example, they can clean parks, help old people buy food, or teach children after school. These activities can make the local area better. Old people may feel less lonely, and streets or parks can be cleaner. Young people can also become more responsible because they understand other people's problems.

However, young people also have study and work. If they spend too much time helping, they may not have enough time for homework or rest. Some teenagers are already very tired because school gives many exams. So community work should not be forced every day.

I think schools and local councils can organise simple activities once or twice a month. Students can choose what kind of help they want to do. This will make them more interested.

In conclusion, young people should help their community, but it should be done in a reasonable way. It is useful for everyone, but it must not make young people too tired. These activities can also teach young people how to speak with adults politely. This is important because many students only talk with friends and do not understand community life.

prompt: Some people think it is better to live in a small town than in a big city. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
sampleText:
Some people prefer to live in a small town, but others like living in a big city. Both places have good points and bad points. I think the better place depends on age, job and family situation.

A small town can be a good place because it is quiet and people may know each other. There is less traffic and the air can be cleaner. Houses may also be cheaper, so families can live in a bigger home. For old people, a small town is comfortable because life is not very fast. Children can also play outside more safely than in a big city.

On the other hand, a big city has more opportunities. There are more jobs, better hospitals, large shopping centres and good schools. Young people can meet many people and enjoy different activities. If someone wants a successful career, a city may be better because there are more companies. Public transport is also usually better.

In my opinion, I prefer living in a big city now because I need work and education. However, I may choose a small town when I am older because it is quieter. Cities should try to reduce noise and pollution, while small towns should improve services.

In conclusion, both places have advantages. People should choose according to their needs, not only because one place is always better. Some people also move between these places during their life. They may live in a city when they are young, and later choose a small town when they want a slower life.

prompt: A friend has invited you to a family celebration, but you cannot attend. Write a letter to your friend. In your letter: thank your friend, explain why you cannot come, and suggest meeting another time.
sampleText:
Dear Maria,

Thank you very much for inviting me to your father's birthday celebration next Saturday. I was very happy to receive your message, and I know this party is important for your family. I would like to come because I have not seen you for a long time.

Unfortunately, I cannot attend the celebration. My sister is moving to a new flat on the same day, and I promised to help her before I got your invitation. She has two young children, so she needs my help with carrying boxes and cleaning the old apartment. It will probably take the whole day, and I cannot leave her alone.

I am sorry to miss the party. Please give my best wishes to your father. I hope he has a wonderful birthday with all the family.

Maybe we can meet the next Sunday afternoon. We could have coffee in the city centre, and you can show me some photos from the celebration.

Best wishes,
Anna

prompt: You borrowed a book from a classmate and damaged it. Write a letter to your classmate. In your letter: apologise, explain what happened, and say how you will fix the problem.
sampleText:
Dear Tom,

I am writing to say sorry about the book I borrowed from you last week. I know you need it for our course, so I feel very bad about what happened.

Yesterday I was reading the book in a cafe after class. I put a cup of coffee on the table, and when I moved my bag, the cup fell over. Some coffee went on the cover and also on three or four pages inside. The book is still possible to read, but it does not look clean now.

I understand this is my mistake. I already checked the price online, and I found the same book in a local shop. I can buy a new copy and give it to you by Friday. If you prefer, I can pay you the money instead, so you can choose where to buy it.

Please accept my apology. I hope this will not cause you too much trouble.

Best regards,
Jack

prompt: Some people believe that everyone should learn to cook at school. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
sampleText:
Some people think every student should learn cooking at school. I mostly agree with this idea because cooking is a useful skill for daily life. Many young people leave home for work or university, but they do not know how to prepare simple food. If schools teach them basic cooking, they can look after themselves better.

One reason is health. People who cannot cook often buy fast food or ready meals. These foods may contain too much oil, sugar or salt. If students learn how to cook rice, vegetables, eggs and simple soup, they may eat healthier meals. They can also save money because cooking at home is usually cheaper than eating outside.

Another reason is responsibility. Cooking lessons can teach students how to plan, clean and work safely. They need to follow instructions and use tools carefully. These are useful life skills, not only kitchen skills.

However, schools already have many important subjects. Cooking should not take too much time from English, maths or science. It could be one practical lesson each week, or a short course in the final year of school.

In conclusion, I think cooking should be taught at school, but it should be simple and practical. Students do not need to become chefs, but they should know how to make basic meals. Cooking can also make students more independent. When they go to university or live away from parents, they will not feel helpless. They can make simple food and avoid spending too much money outside.

prompt: Some people think that advertisements encourage people to buy things they do not need. Do you agree or disagree?
sampleText:
I agree that advertisements often encourage people to buy things they do not really need. Advertising is everywhere, on television, social media, buses and websites. Companies use attractive pictures and strong words to make people feel that a product is necessary, even when it is not.

For example, mobile phone companies advertise new models every year. They say the new phone has a better camera or a faster screen. Many people buy it because they want to look modern, although their old phone still works well. Fashion adverts do the same thing. They make people think last year's clothes are no longer good.

Another reason is that advertisements often use famous people. When young people see a singer or actor using a product, they may want to buy it. They do not always think about whether they need it or can afford it.

However, advertisements can sometimes be useful. They tell customers about new products, discounts or better choices. The problem is that many adverts are designed to create desire, not only to give information.

In conclusion, I believe advertisements can be helpful in some cases, but they often lead people to unnecessary spending. People should think carefully before buying something only because they saw an advert. Schools could also teach young people how advertising works. If students understand that adverts try to influence their emotions, they may become more careful customers in the future. This is why people should compare products by their real needs, not by beautiful pictures or celebrity stories.

prompt: You stayed at a hotel and left something important in your room. Write a letter to the hotel manager. In your letter: say what you left, explain where it might be, and ask the manager to send it to you.
sampleText:
Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing because I think I left my wallet in your hotel room last weekend. I stayed at your hotel from 12 to 14 May, and my room number was 406.

The wallet is black leather and quite small. It has my driving licence, two bank cards and some family photographs inside. There was not much cash, but the cards and licence are very important to me, so I would be grateful if your staff could look for it.

I believe I may have put the wallet in the drawer beside the bed before I went to sleep on my last night. It is also possible that it fell under the bed when I packed my suitcase in the morning.

Could you please ask the housekeeping team to check the room? If they find it, please send it to my home address, which is written below. I will pay for the postage and any other cost.

Thank you for your help.

Yours faithfully,
Michael Brown

prompt: You recently attended a training course and found it useful. Write a letter to your manager. In your letter: describe the course, explain what you learned, and suggest how it could help your team.
sampleText:
Dear Ms Taylor,

I am writing to tell you about the customer service training course I attended last week. The course lasted for two days and was held at the city business centre. It focused on dealing with difficult customers, writing clearer emails and solving complaints more quickly.

I learned several useful ideas from the trainer. The most important one was to listen carefully to the customer's problem before offering a solution. We also practised using polite language when a customer is angry. In addition, the trainer gave us some email examples that can make our replies shorter and more professional.

I think this course could help our whole team. We often receive complaints from customers who are already upset, and sometimes our replies are too long or unclear. If everyone learned these methods, we might solve problems faster and reduce repeat complaints.

Could we invite the same trainer to give a short workshop in our office next month? I believe it would be useful for both new and experienced staff.

Yours sincerely,
Daniel Lee

prompt: Some people think that people should save money for the future, while others think it is better to enjoy life now. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
sampleText:
Some people believe that saving money for the future is very important, while others think people should enjoy life now. Both views have some good reasons, and I think the best choice is to have a balance.

People who support saving money say that the future is uncertain. A person may lose a job, become ill or have to pay for an unexpected problem. If they have savings, they will feel safer and will not need to borrow money. Saving is also useful for big goals such as buying a house, starting a business or paying for children's education. These things usually need careful planning over many years.

On the other hand, some people believe that life should be enjoyed now. They say that nobody knows what will happen in the future, so it is not good to spend all life worrying about money. Travelling, eating out and doing hobbies can make people happy and reduce stress. If people only save, they may miss important experiences with family and friends.

In my opinion, people should save some money but not all of it. It is sensible to put a fixed amount aside each month. After that, they can spend a reasonable amount on things they enjoy. This method gives people both security and happiness.

In conclusion, saving money and enjoying life are both important. A balanced lifestyle is better than only saving or only spending. A further point is that spending habits are difficult to change later. If people become used to buying everything they want, they may struggle when their income becomes lower.

prompt: Some people believe that working from home is better than working in an office. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
sampleText:
Working from home has become more common in many countries. Some people think it is better than working in an office, but others believe the office is still important. I think both ways can be useful, depending on the type of job and the worker's situation.

There are several advantages of working from home. First, employees do not have to travel to work every day. This saves time and money, especially for people who live far from the office. Second, some workers feel more comfortable at home and can concentrate better in a quiet room. They may also have more time with their family because they do not spend hours on the road.

However, working in an office also has benefits. It is easier to talk to colleagues face to face and solve problems quickly. New employees can learn from experienced staff by watching and asking questions. In addition, some people find it difficult to work at home because of children, noise or housework.

In my opinion, a mixed system is the best solution. Employees can work from home for tasks that need concentration, but they can come to the office for meetings and teamwork. This gives people flexibility without losing communication.

In conclusion, working from home and working in an office both have advantages. The best choice depends on the work, so companies should allow some flexibility when possible. Employers also need to consider fairness. Some jobs cannot be done at home, so companies should not give remote workers unfair advantages over staff who must be present.

prompt: A colleague helped you with an important project. Write a letter to your colleague. In your letter: thank them, explain how their help made a difference, and offer to help them in the future.
sampleText:
Dear Sophie,

I wanted to thank you for the help you gave me with the sales report last week. I know you were busy with your own work, so I really appreciate the time you spent checking the figures and improving the presentation slides.

Your suggestions made a big difference. The charts were much clearer after you reorganised them, and the manager said that the report was easy to follow. You also noticed two mistakes in the monthly totals, which could have caused an embarrassing problem during the meeting. Without your help, I would probably have needed another day to finish everything.

Please let me know if you ever need support with one of your projects. I would be very happy to return the favour, especially if you need help with preparing documents or checking data before a deadline.

Thanks again for being so generous with your time. I really enjoyed working with you on this project.

Best regards,
Mark

prompt: Your local library is planning to close on weekends. Write a letter to the library manager. In your letter: explain why weekend opening is important, describe who will be affected, and suggest a solution.
sampleText:
Dear Library Manager,

I am writing about the plan to close the library on weekends. I understand that the library may need to reduce costs, but I believe weekend opening is very important for many local residents.

During the week, many people are at work or school and cannot visit the library. Students often use the library on Saturdays to study in a quiet place, especially when exams are coming. Parents also bring their children at weekends to borrow books and join reading activities. Older residents may be affected as well, because their family members can drive them to the library only on Saturdays or Sundays.

I would like to suggest a compromise. Perhaps the library could open for shorter hours on Saturday, for example from ten in the morning until two in the afternoon. Another possibility is to use volunteers for some weekend activities, while professional staff handle the main desk.

I hope you will consider these ideas before making a final decision.

Yours faithfully,
Laura Chen

prompt: Some people think that governments should spend more money on public parks and sports facilities. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
sampleText:
I agree that governments should spend more money on public parks and sports facilities, although this spending must be balanced with other needs. These places can improve people's health and make communities more pleasant to live in.

The first reason is that parks and sports facilities encourage people to exercise. In many cities, people work long hours and spend too much time sitting. If there is a clean park, a running track or a public sports centre near their home, they may be more likely to walk, play football or do other activities. This can reduce health problems such as obesity and stress. It may also save money for the health system in the long term.

Another benefit is social. Parks are not only places for exercise; they are also places where families, children and older people can meet. A safe playground gives children somewhere to play outside, while benches and gardens provide a relaxing space for older residents. These shared spaces can make people feel more connected to their local area.

However, governments should not spend unlimited money on parks while ignoring hospitals, schools or public transport. In poorer areas, basic services may be more urgent. Therefore, money should be used carefully and parks should be maintained properly after they are built.

In conclusion, I believe public parks and sports facilities are a good investment because they support health and community life. However, this spending should be planned sensibly with other public priorities. Local governments should also listen to residents before building new facilities. If a community needs a playground more than a football field, the money should match local demand.

prompt: Some people believe that parents should teach children how to manage money. Others think schools should do this. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
sampleText:
Some people believe that parents should teach children how to manage money, while others think this should be taught at school. I think both sides have strong points, and the best solution is for parents and schools to share this responsibility.

Parents are important because children learn many daily habits at home. For example, parents can show children how to save part of their pocket money, compare prices in a shop and avoid buying unnecessary things. These lessons are practical and happen in real situations, so children may remember them better. Parents can also explain their own family budget in a simple way, such as how they pay for food, rent and bills.

However, schools can also play an important role. Not all parents have good financial knowledge, and some parents may be too busy to teach these skills. If schools include money management in lessons, every child can learn basic ideas such as budgeting, saving and understanding interest. Teachers can also use activities and examples that are suitable for different ages.

In my opinion, schools should teach the basic principles, while parents should help children practise them at home. For instance, a school lesson can explain how to make a simple budget, and parents can then ask their child to plan spending for a small weekly allowance.

In conclusion, money management is an important life skill. It should not be left only to parents or only to schools, because children need both clear instruction and real practice. This cooperation is important because children need repeated practice. A single school lesson is not enough, but family habits without clear explanation may also be incomplete.
